questions.question: Explain why IKE uses a two-phase process instead of a single negotiation.
expectedPoints: The first phase (Phase 1) establishes a secure, authenticated management channel (IKE SA).
This secure channel then protects the negotiations in the second phase (Phase 2).
Phase 2 negotiates the parameters for the data tunnel (IPsec SA).
This separation provides efficiency, as multiple IPsec SAs can be negotiated under one IKE SA.

questions.question: Compare and contrast IKEv1 and IKEv2, detailing at least three key differences and explaining the significance of one of them.
expectedPoints: IKEv1 is defined by RFC 2409, while IKEv2 is defined by RFC 7296.
IKEv1 has a more complex structure with multiple modes (Main, Aggressive), leading to interoperability issues. IKEv2 is simplified with a single, more robust exchange type.
The initial SA establishment takes 6 messages in IKEv1 Main Mode, but only 4 messages in IKEv2, improving efficiency and connection setup time.
NAT Traversal (NAT-T) was an extension for IKEv1 but is built-in to IKEv2, making VPNs more reliable behind NAT devices.
IKEv2 offers better Denial-of-Service (DoS) protection through built-in cookie mechanisms.
IKEv2 supports the MOBIKE extension for mobile clients, which IKEv1 does not.
